definition: A disposition free from ill will, especially as a component of right attitude.
notes: The project prefers non-ill-will in doctrinal contexts because it stays transparent to the Pāli structure and links cleanly to byāpāda. Goodwill is acceptable in more fluid prose when the phrase would otherwise sound overly technical. The OSF glossary supports this by keeping abyāpāda closely aligned with the path-factor framework rather than broadening it into a vague kindness term.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a smoother English noun is useful

example_phrases.notes: Core link to the path-factor framework.

authority_basis.scope: Named in notes as support for the current abyāpāda policy.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'non-ill-will' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'kindness' or other unrecorded alternates.
